criteria.label: State the k-NN classification and regression prediction rules.
criteria.example: Write: (1) find k training points with smallest d(x, xᵢ); (2) class → majority label; regression → mean of neighbor y values.

criteria.label: Explain why feature scaling is required before distance-based neighbor search.
criteria.example: Describe a 2-feature example where income ($0–500k) overwhelms age (0–100) in L₂ distance without StandardScaler.

criteria.label: Describe how k affects bias and variance of k-NN.
criteria.example: Sketch validation error vs k: small k overfits noise; large k oversmooths; optimal k in between — cite Model Complexity.

criteria.label: Implement or run k-NN with scaled features and report validation accuracy.
criteria.example: Pipeline(StandardScaler, KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5)) on iris or a tabular split; print cross_val_score mean.

criteria.label: Compare k-NN inference cost to an eager model like linear regression.
criteria.example: State training O(1) store vs O(nd) fit for linear model; k-NN query O(n·d) naive vs O(d) fixed predict for linear regression.

criteria.label: Tune k on validation data and justify the chosen value.
criteria.example: Table of k ∈ {1,3,5,...,21} with validation accuracy; pick k=9 because error is minimum and odd k avoids binary ties.
